choice_context: A person is deciding which wine to buy or drink based on the description of the wine.
reasoning: In the context of wine selection, people may disagree on whether they prefer a wine that is more robust and full-bodied versus one that is lighter and more delicate. This disagreement can be influenced by personal taste, dietary restrictions, or the occasion for which the wine is being chosen.

dimensions.name: Body

low_pole.label: Light
low_pole.description: A wine with a subtle flavor profile and a delicate texture.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers light and refreshing wines.

high_pole.label: Full-bodied
high_pole.description: A wine with rich flavors and a robust texture.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys full-bodied and complex wines.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: A 1 might describe a very light and delicate wine, while a 7 would describe a robust and full-bodied wine.

dimensions.name: Acidity

low_pole.label: Low Acidity
low_pole.description: A wine with a gentle and smooth mouthfeel.
low_pole.typical_person: Someone who prefers wines that are not too tart or sharp.

high_pole.label: High Acidity
high_pole.description: A wine with a crisp and tangy mouthfeel.
high_pole.typical_person: Someone who enjoys wines with a bright and zesty character.

dimensions.scoring_guidance: A 1 might describe a wine with very little acidity, while a 7 would describe a wine with a very high level of acidity.

redundancy_check.correlation_note: There is some correlation between body and acidity, as full-bodied wines often have higher acidity levels.
redundancy_check.disentanglement_suggestion: To disentangle these dimensions, consider focusing on the overall mouthfeel and texture for body, and the specific sensation of acidity for acidity. For instance, a wine can be full-bodied but have a lower acidity level, or it can be light but have a high acidity level.
